question text: Imagine hanging wet socks on a clothes hanger. If the hanger tips significantly to one side, what does this indicate about the weight distribution?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The socks are too dry.', 'text': 'The socks are too dry.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The hanger is broken.', 'text': 'The hanger is broken.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The air pressure is higher in the room.', 'text': 'The air pressure is higher in the room.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The magnetic pull is strong.', 'text': 'The magnetic pull is strong.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The plastic is melting.', 'text': 'The plastic is melting.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The weight is not spread evenly along the hanger.', 'text': 'The weight is not spread evenly along the hanger.', 'weight': 100}]
c: The air pressure is higher in the room.
f: The weight is not spread evenly along the hanger.

question text: When a tightrope walker or ice skater extends their arms or leg outwards while moving, what are they attempting to adjust?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'Their fashion style.', 'text': 'Their fashion style.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': "Their body's distribution of mass relative to the center of gravity to prevent falling.", 'text': "Their body's distribution of mass relative to the center of gravity to prevent falling.", 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The temperature of the air around them.', 'text': 'The temperature of the air around them.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The visual color of their performance.', 'text': 'The visual color of their performance.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The speed of the wind.', 'text': 'The speed of the wind.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The friction of the floor.', 'text': 'The friction of the floor.', 'weight': 0}]
b: Their body's distribution of mass relative to the center of gravity to prevent falling.
c: The temperature of the air around them.
d: The visual color of their performance.

question text: In a reflection on a failed design, which comment best represents a "Critical Failure" analysis?
answers: [{'answer_text': '"We didn\'t have enough time."', 'text': '"We didn\'t have enough time."', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': '"The glue smelled funny."', 'text': '"The glue smelled funny."', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': '"I didn\'t like the color of the pipe cleaners."', 'text': '"I didn\'t like the color of the pipe cleaners."', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': '"The project was too hard."', 'text': '"The project was too hard."', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': '"The base was too narrow to support the top-heavy weight distribution."', 'text': '"The base was too narrow to support the top-heavy weight distribution."', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': '"My teammate talked too much."', 'text': '"My teammate talked too much."', 'weight': 0}]
c: "I didn't like the color of the pipe cleaners."
e: "The base was too narrow to support the top-heavy weight distribution."

question text: During a group build, if a disagreement arises about where to place a counterweight, what is the most productive solution?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'Yell until one person gives up.', 'text': 'Yell until one person gives up.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Split the group and build two separate tiny sculptures.', 'text': 'Split the group and build two separate tiny sculptures.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Rock, Paper, Scissors to decide the winner.', 'text': 'Rock, Paper, Scissors to decide the winner.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Test both ideas on the model to see which one creates better equilibrium.', 'text': 'Test both ideas on the model to see which one creates better equilibrium.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'Ask the teacher to solve the problem for you.', 'text': 'Ask the teacher to solve the problem for you.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Stop building and write an essay instead.', 'text': 'Stop building and write an essay instead.', 'weight': 0}]
a: Yell until one person gives up.
b: Split the group and build two separate tiny sculptures.
c: Rock, Paper, Scissors to decide the winner.
d: Test both ideas on the model to see which one creates better equilibrium.
e: Ask the teacher to solve the problem for you.
f: Stop building and write an essay instead.

question text: What is the primary purpose of drawing a sketch or diagram before building a complex structure?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'To waste time before getting the materials.', 'text': 'To waste time before getting the materials.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To ensure you are an artist.', 'text': 'To ensure you are an artist.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To plan the arrangement of materials and predict where the balance points will be.', 'text': 'To plan the arrangement of materials and predict where the balance points will be.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To have a picture to color in later.', 'text': 'To have a picture to color in later.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To show the teacher you can draw circles.', 'text': 'To show the teacher you can draw circles.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To use up the ink in the markers.', 'text': 'To use up the ink in the markers.', 'weight': 0}]
a: To waste time before getting the materials.
b: To ensure you are an artist.
c: To plan the arrangement of materials and predict where the balance points will be.
d: To have a picture to color in later.
e: To show the teacher you can draw circles.
f: To use up the ink in the markers.

question text: When defining "stability" in a structure, which characteristic is most desirable?
answers: [{'answer_text': 'The ability to bounce high.', 'text': 'The ability to bounce high.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The ability to change colors.', 'text': 'The ability to change colors.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The ability to float in water.', 'text': 'The ability to float in water.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The ability to dissolve in acid.', 'text': 'The ability to dissolve in acid.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The ability to return to an upright position after being slightly disturbed.', 'text': 'The ability to return to an upright position after being slightly disturbed.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The ability to conduct electricity.', 'text': 'The ability to conduct electricity.', 'weight': 0}]
c: The ability to float in water.
d: The ability to dissolve in acid.
e: The ability to return to an upright position after being slightly disturbed.

question text: In the "Redesign" phase, engineers often engage in "Iteration." What does this mean?
answers: [{'answer_text': "They copy someone else's work exactly.", 'text': "They copy someone else's work exactly.", 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They write a poem about the project.', 'text': 'They write a poem about the project.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They take the project apart and hide the pieces.', 'text': 'They take the project apart and hide the pieces.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They make small, repeated improvements based on testing results to get a better version.', 'text': 'They make small, repeated improvements based on testing results to get a better version.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'They change the goal of the project to make it easier.', 'text': 'They change the goal of the project to make it easier.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They paint the project a different color and call it done.', 'text': 'They paint the project a different color and call it done.', 'weight': 0}]
a: They copy someone else's work exactly.
b: They write a poem about the project.
c: They take the project apart and hide the pieces.
d: They make small, repeated improvements based on testing results to get a better version.
e: They change the goal of the project to make it easier.
f: They paint the project a different color and call it done.
